From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Scottish trade union

For the union based in Fife, see [Scottish Textile Workers' Union](/wiki/Scottish_Textile_Workers%27_Union "Scottish Textile Workers' Union").

The **Scottish Lace and Textile Workers' Union** was a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") representing textile workers in [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Scottish_Lace_and_Textile_Workers%27_Union&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The union was founded in 1890 as the **Newmilns and District Textile Workers' Union and Friendly Benefit Society**, a local union representing twisthands in the [Newmilns](/wiki/Newmilns "Newmilns") area of [Ayrshire](/wiki/Ayrshire "Ayrshire") in Scotland. It gradually grew, reaching 430 members in 1906, and more than 1,000 by 1910.[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1)

In 1916, the union renamed itself as the **Scottish Lace and Textile Workers' Union**, claiming the right to organise textile workers across Scotland. It did increase its membership to around 2,000, and it joined the British Lace Operatives' Federation. While it tended to dominate the activities of the federation, the English [Amalgamated Society of Operative Lace Makers](/wiki/Amalgamated_Society_of_Operative_Lace_Makers "Amalgamated Society of Operative Lace Makers") was of similar size.[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1) However, in 1971, the Operative Lace Makers merged into the [National Union of Hosiery and Knitwear Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Hosiery_and_Knitwear_Workers "National Union of Hosiery and Knitwear Workers"). The Scottish union decided to remain an independent union, dissolving the federation and affiliating with the [General Federation of Trade Unions](/wiki/General_Federation_of_Trade_Unions_(UK) "General Federation of Trade Unions (UK)") and the [Scottish Trades Union Congress](/wiki/Scottish_Trades_Union_Congress "Scottish Trades Union Congress") (STUC).[[2]](#cite_note-tuh82-2) It was one of the last two Scottish industrial unions affiliated to the STUC, alongside the Scottish Carpet Workers' Union.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

By 1982, the union had only 942 members, based in [Darvel](/wiki/Darvel "Darvel"), [Kilmarnock](/wiki/Kilmarnock "Kilmarnock") and [Newmilns](/wiki/Newmilns "Newmilns").[[2]](#cite_note-tuh82-2) The following year, it merged into the [GMB](/wiki/GMB_(trade_union) "GMB (trade union)"), its former members in 1991 being moved into the union's new Clothing and Textiles Section.[[4]](#cite_note-Waddington-4)

## General Secretaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Scottish_Lace_and_Textile_Workers%27_Union&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: General Secretaries")]

:   George Girvan
:   1935: Robert Calderwood
:   1940: George Girvan
:   1950s: H. Morton
:   1965: W. Dempster
:   1970: Bob Connell
:   1978: Jimmy McChristie
